channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Joe ‘Ferrari’ Sibanyoni and co-accused walk free as case removed from court roll
item.description: The extortion and money laundering case against Mpumalanga minibus taxi boss Joe ‘Ferrari’ Sibanyoni, alongside co-accused Mvimbi Masilela, Philemon Msiza, and Bafana Oupa Sindane, has been struck off...

item.title: Kylie Minogue attends Netflix documentary premiere with ex Jason Donovan decades after Neighbours
item.description: Kylie Minogue attended the London premiere of her new Netflix documentary alongside her former Neighbours co-star and ex-boyfriend Jason Donovan, 36 years after they split. The pair famously played Ch...

item.title: ACT DPP to expand team with 24 new positions in upcoming budget
item.description: The ACT government has announced an $18.9 million boost to the Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) in the upcoming territory budget, aimed at strengthening the office's capacity to man...

item.title: Mazda 3 Review: Comfort, Design and Driving Experience
item.description: The new Mazda 3 continues to uphold the brand’s reputation for comfort, efficiency, and reliability. Driving through the scenic routes of Cape Town, the car feels refined and thoughtfully designed, pr...

item.title: Critics Question DOJ $1.776 Billion Compensation Fund Linked to Trump Allies
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) has established a $1.776 billion taxpayer-funded compensation fund aimed at President Donald Trump's allies, including individuals involved in the January 6 Capito...

item.title: Home Ministry updates CAA rules, requiring passport declaration and surrender for applicants
item.description: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has notified an amendment to the Citizenship Rules regarding the Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA), 2019, effective May 18, 2026. According to the new amendment, appli...

item.title: UK Government Launches High Street Crime Unit to Combat Organised Crime in Retail
item.description: The UK government has announced a £30 million High Street organised crime unit to tackle illegal activity in shops such as mini-marts, vape stores, and barbers, following an extensive year-long BBC in...

item.title: Madina Okot Makes History with First WNBA Double-Double for Atlanta Dream
item.description: Kenya's basketball sensation, Madina Okot, has made a huge statement in the WNBA after an impressive performance against the Las Vegas Aces on Sunday night. At only 21, the Nairobi-born center scored ...

item.title: Renaming PCOS to PMOS: Implications for Women’s Health and Awareness
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) has officially been renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) following over a decade of advocacy and international consultation. This chronic condition,...

item.title: European Military Buildup and the Risks of Escalating Tensions with Russia
item.description: The article discusses current geopolitical tensions in Europe, focusing on Germany's plans to strengthen its military. Berlin aims to transform the Bundeswehr into the strongest conventional army in E...

item.title: Australians from diverse backgrounds discuss support for One Nation amid rising popularity
item.description: Pauline Hanson's One Nation is experiencing growing support across Australia, raising questions about which voters are backing the party and why. Insight spoke to a range of Australians, from migrants...

item.title: Gerald Anderson and Baron Geisler Share Reflections on Past Loves in Stars on Stars Interview
item.description: In a recent episode of the ABS-CBN show 'Stars on Stars,' popular Filipino actors Gerald Anderson and Baron Geisler opened up about their personal experiences with TOTGA or 'The One That Got Away.' Th...

item.title: AFP Maintains High Public Trust and Performance Ratings in Q1 2026 Survey
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) continues to be among the most trusted and well-performing government institutions, according to the Q1 2026 Tugon ng Masa (TNM) Survey conducted by OCTA. The...

item.title: San Diego Islamic Centre shooting leaves three dead; teenage suspects found deceased
item.description: At least five people were killed in a shooting at the Islamic Centre of San Diego in the Clairemont area of California on Monday morning, after two teenage suspects opened fire outside the building. A...

item.title: NBI Chief Denies Allegations of Senate Disruption After Cayetano Claims
item.description: The head of the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has publicly denied claims made by Senator Alan Peter Cayetano that the agency is involved in a plot to destabilize the Senate. Speaking to repor...

item.title: Stolen $24,000 Pinot Noir Returned to Virginia Restaurant Before Court Hearing
item.description: In Virginia, a $24,000 bottle of Pinot Noir that had been stolen from a high-end French restaurant was recovered just before a scheduled court hearing. Sgt. Mike Bell delivered the bottle to the resta...

item.title: Rivers Education Commissioner Urges Media Support for Government Education Programmes
item.description: PORT HARCOURT – The Rivers State Commissioner for Education, Dr. Peters Nwagor, don call on media practitioners for better collaboration to promote government programmes and policies wey fit boost sus...

item.title: Four Italian Divers Found Dead in Maldives Cave After Search Efforts
item.description: Italian authorities confirmed that the bodies of four Italian divers were discovered deep inside an underwater cave in Vaavu Atoll, Maldives, four days after they went missing. The search, which had b...

item.title: Ultra-Glossy Hair: The Latest Trends on Runways
item.description: Reflective, ultra-glossy hair is taking over the fashion runways this season, with designers showcasing styles inspired by wet-look, high-shine textures. At Qasimi, models sported deep, polished finge...

item.title: Nigeria Calls for Strong Measures Against Xenophobic Violence in South Africa
item.description: The xenophobic wahala wey dey South Africa never dey new, but e don dey turn serious matter as e dey affect Nigerians and other African citizens. Recently, two Nigerians and two Ghanaians don die, and...
